summary: The Firefox browser has transitioned its documentation from reStructuredText (RST) format to Markdown, a simpler and more user-friendly format for writing and editing text. This change aims to improve accessibility and ease of maintenance for developers contributing to the documentation, enabling a smoother collaboration experience. With Markdown becoming increasingly popular, this update aligns Firefox's documentation practices with current standards in the developer community, helping ensure that contributors can focus more on content rather than formatting challenges. _While users may not see immediate changes, this shift is significant for the overall development process._
